rulings.Case Title: MAEVE REID et al VS. AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction [C.C.P. 418.10]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2009, line 2. DEFENDANT FARRELL LINES INCORPORATED'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction [C.C.P. 418.10]. Motion to quash granted. Defendant's contacts not substantial, continuous and systematic to demonstrate general jurisdiction. (See DVI, Inc. v. Superior Court (2002) 104 Cal. App. 4th 1080; Cassiar Mining Corp. v. Superior Court (1998) 66 Cal. App. 4th 550.) No demonstration of specific jurisdiction. Plaintiff's cause of action does not arise out of or have a substantial connection with defendant's activities in California. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHRIS V. MATAL SOL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2009, line 6. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. No triable issue of fact raised. Plaintiff could no longer perform the essential functions of his former position as sewer maintenance worker either with or without an accommodation. Plaintiff was not able to perform at least half of the essential functions of a 7215 Supervisor without assistance from someone else (Plaintiff's depo 339-354.) Defendant engaged in timely, good faith interactive process: granted 3 requests for medical leave, temporary modified work duty, and extended search for a suitable open position (UMFs 10, 13-17). No failure to make reasonable accommodation (UMFs 19-20). No showing of vacant positions for which plaintiff qualified and capable of performing with or without accommodation. (UMF 23-24) No showing of temporal proximity between plaintiff's filing of his discrimination claim and his medical separation from his employment. (UMF 32) No showing of severe and pervasive harassment. (UMFs 27-30) Claims of aiding, abetting, inciting, compelling, coercing and failure to take reasonable steps to prevent discrimination and harassment fail with the underlying claims. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JON W ELVIN VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2009, line 16. DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted. A party cannot avoid an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the complaint prior to the hearing. (Sylmar Air Conditioning v. Pueblo Contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal. App. 4th 1049; Simmons v. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001) 92 Cal. App. 4th 1068) Defendant establishes a prima facie showing that the complaint arises out of protected activity. The prosecution of an unlawful detainer action is protected activity within the meaning of CCP section 425.16. Service of a notice to quit is protected activity. (Birkner v. Lam (2007) 156 Cal. App. 4th 275.) The complaint is replete with references to pre-litigation activity. Plaintiff fails to show a probability of prevailing on the merits. The suit is barred by the Government Tort Claims Act. Plaintiff failed to file a claim pursuant to Government Code section 945.4. First Amended complaint dismissed. Defendant to file a motion for fees and costs. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. SANDRA WATSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Renewed Mtn For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2009, line 20. PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Renewed Motion For Summary Judgment. Motion granted. Defendant Sandra Watson inspected her tenant file. (Ruch declaration.) Defendant Watson materially violated the terms of her lease. The notice was properly served. The affirmative defenses are irrelevant to plaintiff's unlawful detainer claim and right to recover possession of the premises. (Baker & Ruch declarations). =(302/CWW)
